turns.name: WILLIAM H. REHNQUIST
turns.text: We'll hear argument now number oh oh seven thirty, Adarand Constructors, Inc. versus Mineta. Mr. Pendley. [Inaudible]

turns.name: WILLIAM P. PENDLEY
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court. In nineteen eighty-nine, the small family business that is Adarand lost a Federal contract because of a racial program and the race of its owner, Randy Pesh. In nineteen ninety-five, this Court held that Adarand had standing to seek forward-looking relief because that program prevented it from competing on an equal footing. In two thousand one, Adarand returns to this Court because it still can't compete on an equal footing. [Inaudible]

turns.name: WILLIAM H. REHNQUIST
turns.text: Mr. Pendley, That...

turns.name: WILLIAM H. REHNQUIST
turns.text: treat, if you will, fairly shortly coming up in your argument, the Government says that in a direct procurement program, these sort of preferences that you're challenging are simply not used in Colorado.

turns.name: WILLIAM P. PENDLEY
turns.text: No, Your Honor.

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: challenged those, as I understand it.

turns.name: WILLIAM P. PENDLEY
turns.text: Your Honor, Adarand has challenged all manner in which the pro-

turns.name: ANTONIN SCALIA
turns.text: But they apply only in the State subsidized programs.

turns.name: WILLIAM P. PENDLEY
turns.text: No, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: In  i-

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: I thought the monetary incentives have been declared unconstitutional by the district court, affirmed by the Tenth Circuit.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Not at all, Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: call they what they call the subcontracting...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Compensation clause, Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: Wel- didn't both courts hold that that was unconstitutional?

turns.name: WILLIAM P. PENDLEY
turns.text: What happened was that the the the district court held that the entire program, all of section eight D of the Sub-Small Business Act, was unconstitutional.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: sufficiently.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: that you were complaining about last time around, that one has been held invalid.

turns.text: It it Your Honor, it is the...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: There are...

turns.text: challenged that.

turns.name: WILLIAM P. PENDLEY
turns.text: Excuse me.

turns.text: No, Your Honor, it is not correct.

turns.text: you just answered my question. You said they...

turns.text: They did not remove the program.

turns.text: Absolutely, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN

turns.text: Yes, Your...

turns.text: Okay now th-

turns.name: UNKNOWN

turns.text: as this Court said in Adarand two thousand, the courts and parties have been confused as to the difference between standing and mootness, and the Tenth Circuit was confused as to mootness and standing, resulting in the Court's Adarand two thousand decision and and it appears to be...

turns.text: Were they at issue...

turns.text: in the suit?

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Yes ma'am. Yes, Your Honor. It's...

turns.name: WILLIAM P. PENDLEY

turns.name: UNKNOWN

turns.text: Jesus God...

turns.name: WILLIAM P. PENDLEY
turns.text: We Your Honor, Adarand had challenged consistently first first of all, the subcontracting compensation clause is one mechanism that the United States developed to implement the statutory program that Adarand challenges.

turns.name: DAVID H. SOUTER

turns.name: WILLIAM P. PENDLEY
turns.text: That Adarand had standing at that time.

turns.text: No.

turns.name: UNKNOWN

turns.name: JUSTICE KENNEDY

turns.text: Okay.

turns.name: WILLIAM P. PENDLEY
turns.text: The reason Adarand does not agree with that premise because the Laidlaw decision holds that once Adarand had standing, as a result of this Court's nineteen ninety-five decision, that standing continued until such time as the United States somehow made the case moot as the result of revocation...

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: Well, I thought Laidlaw stood for the principle that standing is judged as of the time the suit is filed.

turns.name: WILLIAM P. PENDLEY
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor.

turns.name: SANDRA DAY O'CONNOR
turns.text: And subsequent changes affect mootness possibly, but not standing.

turns.name: WILLIAM P. PENDLEY

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG

turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG

turns.text: Your Honor, the the controversy Adarand presents in two thousand one is that Adarand still is unable to compete on an equal footing because the United States still has in its tool of in its tool kit mechanisms by which it is applying this racial preference against Adarand.

turns.text: Mr. Pendley...

turns.text: first rea-

turns.text: Your first point is that the congressional findings are inadequate.
